auto_merge.commit_message:
# Integrate decoupled lookahead warpspeed scan

This is a new scan algorithm based on work by Allard Hendriksen. Chronological summary:

* integrate initial prototype
* update license
* integrate cub warp reductions
* support different input, output and accumulator types
* support different scan operators
* support any problem size and handle partial tiles
* enable exclusive scan and providing an initial value
* support unaligned input and output
* adjust number of stages based on max available smem
* enable PDL
* fallback when compiling for older PTX ISAs and SM arches
* ensure compilation on older CTKs and SM arches
* fallback for non-contiguous inputs, non trivially copyable types, ...
* move hard coded parameters into tuning policies
* ensure forward compatibility of binaries by ensuring at least one stage can run with <=48KiB SMEM, fallback otherwise
* use a single kernel symbol for old and new implementation to limit binary size impact
* rudimentary tuning by adjusting items per thread and number of stages based on input types
* extend support to all compilers supported by CCCL
* partial support for CCCL.C and JIT compilation (needs a bit of follow-up work)
* lots of cleanup
* tons of fixes for corner cases uncovered by CUB & Thrust tests

This commit squashes many individual changes proposed and reviewed on GitLab:

!33 Uses cub cooperative primitives
!34 Cleanup launch code
!35 Add support for different Input / Output types and using the scan operator
!36 Calculate number of stages in dependence of max available smem
!37 Enable exclusive scan and init value
!38 Fix input output types
!39 Add support for unaligned input
!40 Avoid launching the kernel below SM90
!41 Use the different types and initial values for the benchmark
!42 Enable PDL
!43 Fix for non-contiguous iterators
!44 Add support for unaligned output and any problem size
!45 Cleanup alignment tests
!46 Move some hard coded parameters into tuning policies
!48 Manual tuning of tile size
!49 Use MaxPotentialDynamicSmemBytes
!50 Simplify warpspeed values
!52 Prepare a tuning policy for warpspeed and plumb it through
!55 Move squads definition into the warpspeed policy
!57 Check single stage SMEM consumption at compile-time
!58 Use the input tile SMEM for staging the output
!60 Use a single kernel for both scan implementations
!61 Avoid reading garbage in first tile
!62 Fix support for partial tiles
!63 Support atomic loading of >16B types
!64 Properly handle initial value
!66 Simplify final scan
!67 Improve lookahead
!69 Improve lookahead safe
!70 Cleanup loadLookbackTile
!72 Fix a bug in lookback
!73 Fix wrong end index
!74 Fix scan for minimum
!77 Support CCCL.C
!78 Ensure items_per_threads is at least one
!79 Memset tilestate to zero
!82 Limit stages for small problems
!83 Guarantee alignment for output type
!84 Cleanup warpspeed library
!85 Use warpspeed namespace for the warpspeed library
!86 More bulk load & store fixes
!87 Renaming and add a few const
!88 Consistently switch between old/new kernel and fix CCCL.C
!89 Workaround for ptxas bug
!90 Simplify mask computation

Co-authored-by: Allard Hendriksen <ahendriksen@nvidia.com>
Co-authored-by: Michael Schellenberger Costa <miscco@nvidia.com>
